caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 5-digit 54171 role mix is not nanotechnology-specific and is visibly biotech-biased.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The large RESIDUAL mixes materials scientists, engineers, technicians, facilities and administration; 0.25 is a conservative mixed-task judgment.
inputs.pi_dist: No public 541713 revenue split by cost-plus, FTE-rate, fixed-price or grant contract was located.
inputs.pi_dist: Government-services sources are a contract-R&D proxy rather than a code-specific sample.
inputs.pi_moat: This is capture, not the separate physical-work terminal-demand mechanism.
inputs.t50_years: BTOS is an all-business, all-function proxy, not a nanotechnology effective-adoption survey.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Current use in any function is not task-level adoption or a code-specific measurement.
inputs.historical_analogs: Not an observed nanotechnology analogue dataset.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Pure unsupported estimate; validate actual founder age and succession on each target.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of located evidence is not evidence of no succession issue.
inputs.active_consolidators: KPMG’s platform and deal counts cover government technology services, not nanotechnology R&D.
inputs.buy_mult: No public 541713 lower-middle-market transaction-comp set was found.
inputs.buy_mult: Government-contracting valuation commentary is a proxy and may be materially too high for grant-funded or single-PI businesses.
inputs.exit_mult: A grant-dependent or key-person business can exit below entry; selected value is not an observed 541713 exit multiple.
inputs.pricing_structure: NNI and SBIR sources show funding context, not a 541713 revenue-by-contract-form breakdown.
The deterministic 0.0676 labor share is acknowledged as potentially classification-distorted and may materially understate V; the computed V and S are mechanically correct but economically fragile.
B, C, and M rely heavily on disclosed government-services proxies; the consolidator count, contract mix, and 5.5x/6.0x multiples are not observed 541713 market figures.
The DURABLE terminal-demand view is conditional on a fee-for-service physical contract-lab subset; NNI and SBIR evidence shows funding context, not 541713 firm revenue or contract prevalence.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $319,812 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541713); class <5: 608 firms, avg revenue/firm $629,016 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $98,441 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 178 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,590,517 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $248,916 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 150 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,055,340 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $634,661 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 189 firms, avg revenue/firm $11,757,582 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,840,062 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 112 firms, avg revenue/firm $34,040,321 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,327,310 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 232 firms, avg revenue/firm $213,782,134 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $33,456,904 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 301

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Task-based estimate for physical nanotechnology R&D: computational, reporting and administrative work is exposed; synthesis, cleanroom, sample preparation, instrument operation and accountable experimental execution remain.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541713 ($3,606,543,220) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541713 ($56,906,009,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact artifact identity, schemas, dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering, and arithmetic all pass. All six registered sources are accessible and support their stated broad facts, but the anomalous deterministic labor share, non-nanotechnology government-services market proxies, and unmeasured physical contract-lab boundary make the score and terminal thesis economically fragile despite adequate disclosure.

sources.title: North American Industry Classification System — U.S. Census Bureau
sources.what_it_supports: Official 2022 NAICS search result defines 541713 as establishments conducting nanotechnology research and experimental development and describes nanoscale R&D as work on matter at approximately 1–100 nanometers.

sources.title: Large Firms With at Least 20 Employees Biggest AI Users — U.S. Census Bureau
sources.what_it_supports: BTOS reports overall business AI use of 17%–20% between December 2025 and May 2026 and states that its updated supplement covers R&D among 15 business functions.

sources.title: NNI Supplement to the President’s 2025 Budget — National Nanotechnology Coordination Office
sources.what_it_supports: The FY2025 President’s Budget requested $2.2 billion for the National Nanotechnology Initiative across 12 agencies, with more than $45 billion cumulative NNI funding since 2001.

sources.title: SBIR.gov Awards
sources.what_it_supports: The federal SBIR award database publishes downloadable award data and states that award files are refreshed monthly; it establishes that small-business R&D awards are a visible, but not code-specific, funding source.

sources.title: Government Services Update H1 2025 — KPMG Corporate Finance
sources.what_it_supports: KPMG reports 60 government-technology-services deals in H1 2025, 146 PE-backed government-services platforms, procurement uncertainty, and continued selective strategic M&A; this is a government-services proxy, not a 541713 market study.

sources.title: How to Value a Federal Government Contracting Business in 2026 — ExitValue.ai
sources.what_it_supports: Public government-contracting valuation commentary identifies cleared workforce, prime contracts, contract vehicles, DCAA compliance and customer diversification as multiple drivers; used only as a lower-middle-market GovCon valuation proxy.

title: Research and Development in Nanotechnology
